spec_match.rationale: Demo lands on the feature surface — 6 of 8 title keywords plus 9 summary keywords appear in the page.

interactive.rationale: There is a try-it button and some script logic, but the interactive surface is minimal (2 form controls) — adding a live readout or more parameters would push it past the bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb and lede only share 2 keywords — page substance is close but the framing has drifted from how the feature index sells it.

edge_cases.rationale: Some defensive coding present (feature-detect or try/catch), but the happy path is dominant — explicit demos of failure modes would round it out.

references.rationale: Page links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5178789386256384 directly in the see-also / references section.

design_system.rationale: All colours flow through CSS variables, no raw hex, and button overrides keep colour and background in sync.

openQuestions.title: Expand the interactive surface
openQuestions.detail: The page has the bones of an interactive demo but only 2 controls. Add another axis the user can vary (a second parameter, a different input mode, or an explicit before/after toggle) and route the result into a visible readout.

openQuestions.title: Tighten lede / blurb vocabulary alignment
openQuestions.detail: Lede on the concept and blurb on the feature index share only one or two anchor words. Either restate the blurb's promise in the lede, or update the blurb on /v141/support-width-and-height-as-presentation-attributes-on-nested-svg-elements/ to match what the demo actually shows.

openQuestions.title: Add an explicit edge-case branch
openQuestions.detail: The page covers the happy path. Pick at least one failure mode from the spec (missing permission, empty input, unsupported value, rate-limit, etc.) and wire a button that drives the API into that branch with the resulting error / fallback shown in the page.

summary: Hits the bar but several criteria sit at partial — the open questions list concrete additions that would close the gap.
